pr.title: Use Gm_PKind enum for all slot_type values
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced widespread raw slot_type constants with the existing Gm_PKind enum across game-mode, result, player, and fighter call sites. The PR made Player slot-type APIs explicitly use Gm_PKind, changed TmVsData::slot_type to Gm_PKind, documented that PlayerInitData::slot_type stores Gm_PKind values while preserving its u8 storage, and updated many comparisons/assignments from 0/1/2/3 to Gm_PKind_Human, Gm_PKind_Cpu, Gm_PKind_Demo, and Gm_PKind_NA. The decomp.dev bot reported no GALE01 changes, so the edits appear to be type/readability cleanup without binary impact.
what_changed: Converted Player_GetPlayerSlotType and Player_8003248C return types from enum_t to Gm_PKind in player.c/player.h.
Converted Player_SetSlottype parameter type from enum_t to Gm_PKind.
Replaced slot_type literal comparisons and assignments: 0 became Gm_PKind_Human, 1 became Gm_PKind_Cpu, 2 became Gm_PKind_Demo, and 3 became Gm_PKind_NA at many game-mode/result/player call sites.
Changed struct TmVsData slot_type[4] from s32 to Gm_PKind in src/melee/gm/types.h.
Updated PlayerInitData::slot_type comment in src/melee/mn/types.h from an uncertain numeric comment to 'uses Gm_PKind values' while leaving the field as u8.
Added/adjusted forward includes so files using Gm_PKind can see the player forward declaration, including src/melee/gm/types.h and src/melee/gm/gm_17AD.c.
Incidental cleanup in ftbosslib.c replaced a manual OSReport/__assert block with HSD_ASSERTREPORT, used sqrtf instead of a local my_sqrtf helper, and used GET_FIGHTER for gobj user data.
Removed several SortPriority entries from .clang-format IncludeCategories, affecting include regrouping behavior.
smart_moves: Made the semantic mapping of slot types explicit everywhere instead of relying on fragile magic numbers.
Strengthened API boundaries by making player slot-type getters/setters use Gm_PKind directly, which encourages future call sites to keep the enum rather than cast through generic enum_t.
Preserved known ABI/storage where evidence indicates it matters: PlayerInitData::slot_type remained u8 but was documented as containing Gm_PKind values.
Typed aggregate storage where safer: TmVsData::slot_type became Gm_PKind rather than s32.
Used broad mechanical replacement across all visible slot_type users, reducing the chance of mixed numeric/enum style in the same subsystem.
Kept the PR binary-safe according to the decomp.dev report: 'No changes' for GALE01.
decomp_lessons: When an enum already explains a repeated numeric protocol, convert comparisons, assignments, and function signatures together; partial conversion leaves reviewers and future agents guessing which literals are still intentional.
Do not blindly widen/narrow struct fields during semantic cleanup. A field can remain byte-sized for layout while its comment documents that it stores enum values.
For decomp readability PRs, a successful 'No changes' decomp.dev report is important evidence that type/comment/include cleanup preserved matching.
Forward declarations and include hygiene matter when moving a type like Gm_PKind into public signatures; add the smallest needed forward include rather than forcing full headers everywhere.
Magic numbers in result/game-mode code often encode shared player concepts; prefer the shared game/player enum names so later decompilation can reason across files.
naming_conventions: Slot-type enum values use the Gm_PKind_* prefix, including Gm_PKind_Human, Gm_PKind_Cpu, Gm_PKind_Demo, Gm_PKind_Boss, and Gm_PKind_NA.
Player slot-type accessors retain existing project names: Player_GetPlayerSlotType, Player_8003248C, and Player_SetSlottype.
Fields named slot_type should be treated as Gm_PKind-valued even when the storage type remains u8 for struct layout.
The PR consistently capitalizes Cpu as Gm_PKind_Cpu, not CPU.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Enum substitution for integer literals can be matching-neutral when the underlying values stay the same.
Changing function prototypes from enum_t to a specific enum type can improve type checking without necessarily changing generated code.
Keeping PlayerInitData::slot_type as u8 avoided a likely struct layout/ABI change while still documenting the enum semantics.
The decomp.dev bot comment reported 'No changes' for GALE01, supporting that the cleanup did not alter the target binary.
Incidental ftbosslib cleanup used standard project/library helpers such as HSD_ASSERTREPORT, sqrtf, and GET_FIGHTER while preserving matching according to the bot report.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only issue comment was from decomp-dev[bot], reporting: 'Report for GALE01 (91a235a - e94d5d6): No changes.'
follow_up_queries: Find remaining numeric comparisons against slot_type that should become Gm_PKind values.
Audit structs with slot_type fields to decide whether storage should remain u8 or become Gm_PKind.
Search for enum_t player APIs that can be narrowed to named enums without codegen changes.
Check whether other player-kind constants in comments still use numeric descriptions like 0 = human, 1 = cpu.
Review include dependencies around Gm_PKind after adding it to public player and game-mode headers.
